title: mentha × rotundifolia subsp. oblongifolia
intro: Mentha × rotundifolia subsp. oblongifolia, also known as Welsh pepper or round-leaved mint, is a subspecies of the peppermint plant, characterized by its distinctive leaf shape and growth habit. The medicinal properties of Mentha × rotundifolia subsp. oblongifolia are attributed to its high menthol content, which has been used to treat digestive issues, headaches, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to ease nausea, reduce inflammation, and improve digestion, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of Mentha × rotundifolia subsp. oblongifolia include teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used topically or internally to promote overall well-being.
